title: Wearable Device-Based Real-Time Monitoring of Physiological Signals: Evaluating Cognitive Load Across Different Tasks
abstract: arXiv:2406.07147v3 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: This study employs cutting-edge wearable monitoring technology to conduct high-precision, high-temporal-resolution (1-second interval) cognitive load assessment on electroencephalogram (EEG) data from the FP1 channel and heart rate variability (HRV) data of secondary vocational students. By jointly analyzing these two critical physiological indicators, the research delves into their application value in assessing cognitive load among secondary vocational students and their utility across various tasks. The study designed two experiments to validate the efficacy of the proposed approach: Initially, a random forest classification model, developed using the N-BACK task, enabled the precise decoding of physiological signal characteristics in secondary vocational students under different levels of cognitive load, achieving a classification accuracy of 97%. Subsequently, this classification model was applied in a cross-task experimen

translated_title: 基于可穿戴设备的生理信号实时监测：评估不同任务下的认知负荷
translated_abstract: 本研究采用前沿的可穿戴监测技术，对中职学生的FP1通道脑电图数据和心率变异性数据进行高精度、高时间分辨率（1秒间隔）的认知负荷评估。通过联合分析这两个关键生理指标，研究深入探讨了它们在中职学生认知负荷评估中的应用价值及在不同任务中的效用。研究设计了两个实验来验证所提方法的有效性：首先，利用N-BACK任务开发的随机森林分类模型，实现了对中职学生在不同认知负荷水平下生理信号特征的精确解码，分类准确率达到97%。随后，该分类模型被应用于跨任务实验中。
tldr: 本研究利用可穿戴设备联合分析脑电图和心率变异性数据，通过随机森林分类模型实现了中职学生认知负荷的高精度实时监测，准确率达97%，并验证了其在跨任务场景中的有效性。
en_tdlr: This study uses wearable devices to jointly analyze EEG and HRV data, achieving high-precision real-time cognitive load monitoring for secondary vocational students with 97% accuracy via a random forest model, and validates its effectiveness in cross-task scenarios.
